title.zh: 卷六十三 武五子傳
title.en: Volume 63: The five sons of Emperor Wu
title.raw: 卷六十三 武五子傳 Volume 63: The five sons of Emperor Wu

sentences.zh: 卷六十三 武五子傳第三十三

translations.literal: Scroll sixty-three, Treatise thirty-three, the Five Sons of Emperor Wu.
translations.idiomatic: Chapter 63: Biographies of the Five Sons of Emperor Wu.

sentences.zh: 孝武皇帝六男。

translations.literal: The Filially Martial Emperor had six sons.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu had six sons.

sentences.zh: 衛皇后生戾太子，趙婕妤生孝昭帝，王夫人生齊懷王閎，李姬生燕刺王旦、廣陵厲王胥，李夫人生昌邑哀王髆。

translations.literal: The Empress Wei bore the Crown Prince Li, Ju; Lady Zhao the Worthy bore Emperor Xiaozhao; Lady Wang bore Prince Huai of Qi, Hong; Lady Li bore Prince Ci of Yan, Dan, and Prince Li of Guangling, Xu; Lady Li bore Prince Ai of Changyi, Bo.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Wei gave birth to Crown Prince Ju, known posthumously as the Prince of Li; Lady Zhao the Worthy bore Emperor Zhao; Lady Wang bore Liu Hong, Prince Huai of Qi; Lady Li bore Liu Dan, Prince of Yan, and Liu Xu, Prince Li of Guangling; Lady Li bore Liu Bo, Prince Ai of Changyi.

sentences.zh: 戾太子據

translations.literal: The Crown Prince Li, Ju.
translations.idiomatic: Crown Prince Ju (posthumously styled the Prince of Li).

sentences.zh: 戾太子據，元狩元年立為皇太子，年七歲矣。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince Li, Ju, in the first year of Yuanshou was established as crown prince; he was seven years old.
translations.idiomatic: Crown Prince Ju was named heir apparent in the first year of the Yuanshou era (122 B.C.); he was seven at the time.

sentences.zh: 初，上年二十九乃得太子，甚喜，為立禖，使東方朔、枚皋作禖祝。

translations.literal: At first, when the Emperor was twenty-nine he finally obtained the crown prince; he was greatly pleased, and for him established a prayer-for-sons shrine, and ordered Dongfang Shuo and Mei Gao to compose the prayer invocations.
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor, at twenty-nine, finally had an heir, he was overjoyed. He founded a shrine to pray for descendants and commissioned Dongfang Shuo and Mei Gao to write the dedicatory texts.

sentences.zh: 少壯，詔受《公羊春秋》，又從瑕丘江公受《穀梁》。

translations.literal: In youth and prime, by edict he received instruction in the Gongyang Commentary on the Spring and Autumn Annals, and also followed Jiang Gong of Xiaqiu in the Guliang tradition.
translations.idiomatic: As he grew, he was ordered to study the Gongyang Commentary on the Spring and Autumn Annals, then took the Guliang tradition from Jiang Gong of Xiaqiu.

sentences.zh: 及冠就宮，上為立博望苑，使通賓客，從其所好，故多以異端進者。

translations.literal: When he capped and entered his palace, the Emperor for him established Broad Vista Park, allowing him to receive guests and follow his inclinations; therefore many heterodox persons were presented.
translations.idiomatic: After his capping he moved into his own residence, and the emperor built Broad Vista Park for him so he could host scholars and pursue his interests—which is why so many unconventional figures found their way to his court.

sentences.zh: 元鼎四年，納史良娣，產子男進，號曰史皇孫。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Yuanding, he took Lady Shi as a worthy concubine, who produced a son named Jin, styled the Imperial Grandson of the Shi line.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of Yuanding (113 B.C.), he took Consort Shi; she bore a son named Jin, who was known as the Imperial Grandson of the Shi line.

sentences.zh: 武帝末，衛后寵衰，江充用事，充與太子及衛氏有隙，恐上晏駕後為太子所誅，會巫蠱事起，充因此為奸。

translations.literal: Near the end of Emperor Wu's reign, Empress Wei's favor declined, Jiang Chong held power; Chong had a rift with the crown prince and the Wei clan, and feared that after the Emperor's carriage returned late he would be executed by the crown prince; when the witchcraft affair arose, Chong on this account acted wickedly.
translations.idiomatic: Late in Emperor Wu's reign, Empress Wei fell from favor while Jiang Chong rose to power. Chong had quarreled with the heir and the Wei family, and he dreaded what would happen once the old emperor died. When the witchcraft panic broke out, he seized the chance to advance his own schemes.

sentences.zh: 是時，上春秋高，意多所惡，以為左右皆為蠱道祝詛，窮治其事。

translations.literal: At that time the Emperor's years were high, his mind had many things it detested, and he thought those at his side all practiced witchcraft cursing; he exhaustively investigated the affair.
translations.idiomatic: By then the emperor was old, suspicious, and convinced that everyone around him was dabbling in sorcery and laying curses. He ordered a pitiless inquiry.

sentences.zh: 丞相公孫賀父子，陽石、諸邑公主，及皇后弟子長平侯衛伉皆坐誅。

translations.literal: Prime Minister Gongsun He and his son, the Princesses of Yangshi and Zhuyi, and the empress's nephew Marquis of Changping Wei Kan all sat guilt and were executed.
translations.idiomatic: Prime Minister Gongsun He and his son, Princesses Yangshi and Zhuyi, and the empress's nephew Wei Kan, marquis of Changping, were all condemned and put to death.

sentences.zh: 語在《公孫賀》、《江充傳》。

translations.literal: The words are in the 'Gongsun He' and 'Jiang Chong' treatises.
translations.idiomatic: The full account appears in the biographies of Gongsun He and Jiang Chong.

sentences.zh: 充典治巫蠱，既知上意，白言宮中有蠱氣，入宮至省中，壞御座掘地。

translations.literal: Chong directed the handling of witchcraft; once he knew the Emperor's intent, he reported white that there was witchcraft vapor in the palace; he entered the palace to the inner halls, broke the imperial seat and dug the ground.
translations.idiomatic: Put in charge of the witchcraft investigation, Jiang Chong soon sensed what the emperor wanted to hear. He reported an evil aura inside the palaces, then went into the residential quarters, smashed the imperial dais, and began digging up the floors.

sentences.zh: 上使按道侯韓說、御史章贛、黃門蘇文等助充。

translations.literal: The Emperor sent Marquis of Andao Han Yue, Censor Zhang Gan, Yellow Gate Su Wen, and others to assist Chong.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor sent Han Yue, marquis of Andao, Censor Zhang Gan, the palace attendant Su Wen, and others to assist Jiang Chong.

sentences.zh: 充遂至太子宮掘蠱，得桐木人。

translations.literal: Chong then went to the crown prince's palace to dig for witchcraft, and obtained a paulownia-wood man.
translations.idiomatic: Jiang Chong proceeded to the heir's residence to search for charms and found a straw effigy buried in the ground—as if by design.

sentences.zh: 時上疾，辟暑甘泉宮，獨皇后、太子在。

translations.literal: At the time the Emperor was ill, avoiding summer heat at Sweet Springs Palace; only the empress and crown prince remained.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was ill and had withdrawn to Sweet Springs to escape the heat, leaving only the empress and the crown prince in the capital.

sentences.zh: 太子召問少傅石德，德懼為師傅並誅，因謂太子曰：「前丞相父子、兩公主及衛氏皆坐此，今巫與使者掘地得征驗，不知巫置之邪，將實有也，無以自明，可矯以節收捕充等系獄，窮治其奸詐。

translations.literal: The crown prince summoned and questioned Junior Tutor Shi De; De feared that tutor and tutee together would be executed, and therefore said to the crown prince: 'Formerly the prime minister and his son, the two princesses, and the Wei clan all sat this; now the witchcraft men and envoys dig the ground and obtain verification; we do not know whether the witchcraft was placed or is really there; there is no means to clear ourselves; you may falsely use the tally to seize and capture Chong and others, bind them in prison, and exhaustively investigate their treachery.'
translations.idiomatic: The crown prince consulted Junior Tutor Shi De. De feared they would execute tutor and pupil together, so he urged: The late chancellor and his son, the two princesses, and the Wei clan were all destroyed over such charges. Now these investigators dig up supposed proof underground—but we cannot tell whether the dolls were planted or are genuine. You have no way to clear your name except to use the imperial tally, arrest Jiang Chong and his men, throw them in jail, and force a confession out of them.

sentences.zh: 且上疾在甘泉，皇后及家吏請問皆不報，上存亡未可知，而奸臣如此，太子將不念秦扶蘇事耶？」

translations.literal: Moreover the Emperor's illness is at Sweet Springs; the empress and household clerks ask and are all not answered; whether the Emperor lives or dies is not yet known; yet wicked ministers are thus; will the crown prince not recall the affair of Qin's Fusu?'
translations.idiomatic: 'Besides, the emperor lies ill at Sweet Springs, and neither the empress nor her household has been able to get word back from the court—no one knows whether he is alive or dead. Yet villains act like this. Will you not remember how the First Emperor's son Fusu was destroyed?'

sentences.zh: 太子急，然德言。

translations.literal: The crown prince was pressed and approved De's words.
translations.idiomatic: Cornered, the crown prince accepted Shi De's counsel.

sentences.zh: 征和二年七月壬午，乃使客為使者收捕充等。

translations.literal: In the second year of Zhenghe, seventh month, day renwu, then he sent a retainer as envoy to seize and capture Chong and others.
translations.idiomatic: On the renwu day of the seventh month in the second year of Zhenghe (91 B.C.), he sent agents disguised as imperial messengers to arrest Jiang Chong and his associates.

sentences.zh: 按道侯說疑使者有詐，不肯受詔，客格殺說。

translations.literal: Marquis of Andao Han Yue suspected the envoy had fraud, refused to receive the edict; the retainer struck and killed Yue.
translations.idiomatic: Han Yue of Andao suspected the envoys were impostors and refused to hand himself over; one of the crown prince's men killed him.

sentences.zh: 御史章贛被創突亡。

translations.literal: Censor Zhang Gan, wounded, suddenly fled.
translations.idiomatic: Censor Zhang Gan, wounded, broke away and fled.

sentences.zh: 自歸甘泉。

translations.literal: He himself returned to Sweet Springs.
translations.idiomatic: He made his own way to Sweet Springs to report.

sentences.zh: 太子使舍人無且持節夜入未央宮殿長秋門，因長御倚華具白皇后，發中廄車載射士，出武庫兵，發長樂宮衛，告令百官日江充反。

translations.literal: The crown prince sent his retainer Wu Qian holding the tally by night to enter Weiyang Palace's Changle Gate; through Senior Attendant Yi Hua he fully informed the empress, issued central-stable chariots carrying archers, issued weapons from the arsenal, issued guards of Changle Palace, and proclaimed to the hundred officials that Jiang Chong had rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: The crown prince sent his attendant Wu Qian, tally in hand, into Weiyang Palace through the Changle Gate in the dead of night. Through Senior Attendant Yi Hua he explained everything to the empress, then requisitioned chariots from the central stables, arms from the arsenal, and guards from Changle Palace, and proclaimed to the officials that Jiang Chong had risen in revolt.

sentences.zh: 乃斬充以徇，炙胡巫上林中。

translations.literal: Then he beheaded Chong to display, and roasted the Hu shamans in Shanglin.
translations.idiomatic: Jiang Chong was executed and his head displayed in public; the foreign sorcerers were burned alive in Shanglin Park.

sentences.zh: 遂部賓客為將率，與丞相劉屈氂等戰。

translations.literal: Then he arrayed guests as generals and led troops, and fought with Prime Minister Liu Qumao and others.
translations.idiomatic: He organized his followers into an armed force and clashed with Chancellor Liu Qumao and government troops.

sentences.zh: 長安中擾亂，言太子反，以故眾不附。

translations.literal: Within Chang'an there was turmoil; it was said the crown prince had rebelled; therefore the masses did not attach.
translations.idiomatic: Chang'an descended into chaos; rumor said the heir had turned traitor, so the people would not rally to him.

sentences.zh: 太子兵敗，亡，不得。

translations.literal: The crown prince's troops were defeated; he fled and could not be obtained.
translations.idiomatic: The crown prince's army was routed; he slipped into hiding and could not be found.

sentences.zh: 上怒甚，群下憂懼，不知所出。

translations.literal: The Emperor was extremely angry; those below were anxious and fearful, not knowing what to do.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor's rage terrified the whole court; no one knew what to say.

sentences.zh: 壺關三老茂上書曰：「臣聞父者猶天，母者猶地，子猶萬物也。

translations.literal: Mao, the village elder of Huguan, submitted a memorial saying: 'Your servant has heard the father is like Heaven, the mother like Earth, the child like the myriad things.'
translations.idiomatic: Mao, the village elder of Huguan, presented a memorial: 'I have heard it said that the father is like Heaven, the mother like Earth, and the child like the myriad creatures that depend on them.'

sentences.zh: 故天平地安，陰陽和調，物乃茂成；

translations.literal: Therefore when Heaven is level and Earth secure, yin and yang harmonize and adjust, then things flourish and complete;
translations.idiomatic: When Heaven holds steady and Earth lies calm, and yin and yang move in harmony, every living thing thrives.

sentences.zh: 父慈母愛，室家之中子乃孝順。

translations.literal: the father compassionate and the mother loving, then within the chamber the child is filial and compliant.
translations.idiomatic: When a father is kind and a mother loving, children within the household embody true filial duty.

sentences.zh: 陰陽不和，則萬物夭傷；

translations.literal: When yin and yang are not harmonious, then the myriad things die young and are injured;
translations.idiomatic: When yin and yang fall out of balance, creation withers before its time.

sentences.zh: 父子不和，則室家喪亡。

translations.literal: when father and son are not harmonious, then the chamber and family perish and are lost.
translations.idiomatic: When father and son are at odds, the home itself is undone.

sentences.zh: 故父不父則子不子，君不君則臣不臣，雖有粟，吾豈得而食諸！

translations.literal: Therefore if the father is not a father then the son is not a son, if the lord is not a lord then the minister is not a minister; though there be grain, how could I get to eat it!'
translations.idiomatic: Hence the saying: if a father fails as a father, his son cannot be a true son; if a ruler fails as a ruler, his ministers cannot be loyal ministers—and though the granaries overflow, who could bring himself to eat?'

sentences.zh: 昔者虞舜，孝之至也，而不中於瞽叟；

translations.literal: In olden times Yu Shun was utmost in filial piety, yet did not hit the mark with Blind Gusou;
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity Shun of Yu was the very model of filial piety, yet even he could not win over his blind father Gusou;

sentences.zh: 孝已被謗，伯奇放流，骨肉至親，父子相疑。

translations.literal: Xiao Ji was slandered, Bo Qi was banished and drifted away; flesh and bone utmost kin, yet father and son suspected each other.
translations.idiomatic: Xiao Ji was maligned, Bo Qi was driven into exile—bone of their fathers' bone—yet father and son came to doubt each other.

sentences.zh: 何者？

translations.literal: What was the reason?
translations.idiomatic: Why?

sentences.zh: 積毀之所生也。

translations.literal: It is what accumulated slander produces.
translations.idiomatic: Because slander, piled up long enough, is eventually believed.

sentences.zh: 由是觀之，子無不孝，而父有不察，今皇太子為漢適嗣，承萬世之業，體祖宗之重，親則皇帝之宗子也。

translations.literal: From this viewing it, the son is not lacking filiality, yet the father has failure to examine; now the imperial crown prince is the Han lawful heir, inheriting the enterprise of ten thousand generations, embodying the weight of the ancestors; in kin he is the Emperor's eldest-son grandson.
translations.idiomatic: Seen in this light, sons seldom lack devotion—it is fathers who sometimes fail to look clearly. The crown prince is the lawful heir of Han, the man who must carry the ancestral mandate across endless generations; in blood he is the emperor's own firstborn son.

sentences.zh: 江充，布衣之人，閭閻之隸臣耳，陛下顯而用之，銜至尊之命以迫蹴皇太子，造飾奸詐，群邪錯謬，是以親戚之路隔塞而不通。

translations.literal: Jiang Chong, a man of common cloth, a bondservant of the lanes and alleys; yet Your Majesty displayed and employed him; bearing the supreme command he pressed and drove the imperial crown prince, forging and adorning treachery; the crowd of evils was wrong and confused; therefore the road of kin was blocked and plugged and did not pass.
translations.idiomatic: Jiang Chong was a nobody from the back alleys, yet Your Majesty raised him to power. Wielding authority as though it were your own, he hounded the heir, inventing crimes and spreading lies until honest kin could no longer reach your ear.

sentences.zh: 太子進則不得上見，退則困於亂臣，獨冤結而亡告，不忍忿忿之心，起而殺充，恐懼逋逃，子盜父兵以救難自免耳，臣竊以為無邪心。

translations.literal: The crown prince advancing then could not obtain audience above; retreating then was trapped by rebellious ministers; alone with grievance tied and nowhere to announce it, unable to bear resentful heart, he rose and killed Chong; fearing, he fled; the son stole the father's troops to save difficulty and free himself; your servant privately thinks there was no evil heart.
translations.idiomatic: The crown prince could not win an audience above and was hemmed in by corrupt ministers below; his wrongs had nowhere to go. Unable to swallow his rage, he struck down Jiang Chong and fled in terror—he merely borrowed his father's soldiers to break a trap, not to seize the throne. This subject believes he harbored no treasonous intent.

sentences.zh: 《詩》曰：『營營青蠅，止於籓；

translations.literal: The Odes says: 'Buzzing buzzing green flies, stop at the fence;'
translations.idiomatic: The Book of Odes says: 'Buzzing green flies light on the fence.'

sentences.zh: 愷悌君子，無信讒言；

translations.literal: 'Joyful and easy gentlemen, do not trust slanderous words;'
translations.idiomatic: 'Gentle, easygoing gentlemen, do not trust slanderous words.'

sentences.zh: 讒言罔極，交亂四國。』

translations.literal: 'Slanderous words without limit, together throw the four states into chaos.'
translations.idiomatic: 'Slander knows no limit; it can throw whole kingdoms into turmoil.'

sentences.zh: 往者江充讒殺趙太子，天下莫不聞，其罪固宜。

translations.literal: In the past Jiang Chong by slander killed the Zhao crown prince; all under Heaven heard it; his guilt solidly deserved it.
translations.idiomatic: Everyone knows how Jiang Chong once slandered the heir of Zhao to his death—his guilt was plain.

sentences.zh: 陛下不省察，深過太子，發盛怒，舉大兵而求之，三公自將，智者不敢言，辯士不敢說，臣竊痛之。

translations.literal: Your Majesty did not examine and inspect, deeply faulted the crown prince, issued great anger, raised great troops to seek him; the Three Dukes personally led; the wise dared not speak, the rhetoricians dared not explain; your servant privately is pained by it.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Your Majesty did not look into the matter; you heaped blame on the heir and unleashed full armies in pursuit, with the Three Dukes leading the host in person. Wise men held their tongues; counselors dared not speak. Your servant is heartsick over it.

sentences.zh: 臣聞子胥盡忠而忘其號，比幹盡仁而遺其身，忠臣竭誠不顧鈇鉞之誅以陳其愚，志在匡君安社稷也。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard Zixu exhausted loyalty and forgot his title, Bigan exhausted benevolence and cast away his body; loyal ministers exhaust sincerity, not looking to axe and halberd execution, to state their foolishness; their intent lies in correcting the lord and securing the altars of soil and grain.
translations.idiomatic: I recall how Wu Zixu gave everything to loyalty and lost his life for it, how Bigan gave everything to goodness and died for it. A loyal subject will speak blunt truth though it cost him the ax—because his only purpose is to steady the throne and preserve the realm.

sentences.zh: 《詩》云：『取彼譖人，投畀豺虎。』

translations.literal: The Odes says: 'Take those slanderers, throw them to wolves and tigers.'
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says: 'Take those slanderers and throw them to the wolves and tigers.'

sentences.zh: 唯陛下寬心慰意，少察所親，毋患太子之非，亟罷甲兵，無令太子久亡。

translations.literal: Only may Your Majesty broaden the heart and soothe the intent, slightly examine those you are kin to, do not worry about the crown prince's wrongdoing, urgently dismiss armour and weapons, do not let the crown prince long flee.
translations.idiomatic: I beg you to ease your mind, look again at those closest to you, and not dwell on the heir's missteps. Order the armies to stand down before your son remains a fugitive any longer.

sentences.zh: 臣不勝惓惓，出一旦之命，待罪建章闕下。」

translations.literal: Your servant cannot overcome earnest devotion, brings out a single morning's life, awaits punishment beneath the gates of Jianzhang Palace.'
translations.idiomatic: I stake my single life on these words and await your judgment beneath the gates of Jianzhang Palace.'

sentences.zh: 書奏，天子感寤。

translations.literal: When the writing was submitted, the Son of Heaven was moved and awakened.
translations.idiomatic: When the memorial reached him, the emperor was shaken into clarity.

sentences.zh: 太子之亡也，東至湖，臧匿泉鳩裡。

translations.literal: When the crown prince fled, east he reached Hu, and hid at Huguali by the Quanjiu spring.
translations.idiomatic: While the crown prince was on the run, he fled east to Hu County and took refuge at Huguali by the spring.

sentences.zh: 主人家貧，常賣屨以給太子。

translations.literal: The master of the house was poor, often selling sandals to supply the crown prince.
translations.idiomatic: His host was destitute and often sold sandals just to feed him.

sentences.zh: 太子有故人在湖，聞其富贍，使人呼之而發覺。

translations.literal: The crown prince had an old acquaintance in Hu, heard he was rich and ample, sent a man to summon him and was discovered.
translations.idiomatic: The heir had a former friend in Hu who had grown wealthy; when he sent someone to call on him, the plot was exposed.

sentences.zh: 吏圍捕太子，太子自度不得脫，即入室距戶自經。

translations.literal: Officials surrounded and captured the crown prince; the crown prince himself reckoned he could not escape, then entered the chamber, barred the door, and hanged himself.
translations.idiomatic: Troops closed in. Seeing no way out, he went inside, barred the door, and hanged himself.

sentences.zh: 山陽男子張富昌為卒，足蹋開戶，新安令史李壽趨抱解太子，主人公遂格鬥死，皇孫二人皆並遇害。

translations.literal: The man of Shanyang Zhang Fuchang being a soldier, foot trampled open the door; the sheriff of Xin'an Li Shou hurried, embraced and released the crown prince; the master of the house then fought and died; the two imperial grandsons both together met harm.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Fuchang, a foot soldier from Shanyang, kicked the door open. Li Shou, a clerk from Xin'an, rushed in, seized the dangling body, and lowered the rope. The master of the house was killed in the melee, and the two young grandsons who had fled with the prince died as well.

sentences.zh: 上既傷太子，乃下詔曰：「蓋行疑賞，所以申信也。

translations.literal: The Emperor having grieved the crown prince, then issued an edict saying: 'In general, doubtful reward is practiced, thereby to extend trust.'
translations.idiomatic: Grieving for the crown prince, the emperor issued an edict: 'Even when merit is uncertain, reward must sometimes be granted, so that good faith may be made clear.'

sentences.zh: 其封李壽為干阜侯，張富昌為題侯。」

translations.literal: Enfeoff Li Shou as Marquis of Qianfu, Zhang Fuchang as Marquis of Ti.'
translations.idiomatic: Let Li Shou be enfeoffed as marquis of Qianfu, and Zhang Fuchang as marquis of Ti.'

sentences.zh: 久之，巫蠱事多不信。

translations.literal: After a long time, much of the witchcraft affair was not believed.
translations.idiomatic: As time passed, fewer and fewer people credited the witchcraft charges.

sentences.zh: 上知太子惶恐無他意，而車千秋復訟太子冤，上遂擢千秋為丞相，而族滅江充家，焚蘇文於橫橋上，及泉鳩裡加兵刃於太子者，初為北地太守，後族。

translations.literal: The Emperor knew the crown prince was frightened and had no other intent, and Che Qianqiu again sued the crown prince's grievance; the Emperor then promoted Qianqiu as prime minister, and exterminated Jiang Chong's clan, burned Su Wen on Hengqiao Bridge, and as for those who at Huguali applied weapons to the crown prince, at first he was governor of Beidi, later exterminated the clan.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor came to see that the heir had acted only out of panic, not rebellion. When Che Qianqiu again pleaded the prince's cause, he promoted Qianqiu to chancellor, wiped out Jiang Chong's family, burned Su Wen alive at Hengqiao Bridge, and executed even the official who had first governed Beidi and later led the raid at Huguali.

sentences.zh: 上憐太子無辜，乃作思子宮，為歸來望思之台於湖。

translations.literal: The Emperor pitied the crown prince's innocence, then built the Palace for Mourning the Son, and for him built the Terrace of Return and Longing at Hu.
translations.idiomatic: Moved by his son's innocence, he built the Palace for Mourning the Crown Prince and raised the Terrace of Return and Longing at Hu.

sentences.zh: 天下聞而悲之。

translations.literal: All under Heaven heard and grieved him.
translations.idiomatic: Throughout the realm, people wept when they heard of it.

sentences.zh: 初，太子有三男一女，女者平輿侯嗣子尚焉。

translations.literal: At first the crown prince had three sons and one daughter; the daughter, the heir son of Marquis of Pingyu married her.
translations.idiomatic: The crown prince had left three sons and a daughter; the girl had married the heir of the marquis of Pingyu.

sentences.zh: 及太子敗，皆同時遇害。

translations.literal: When the crown prince was defeated, all together met harm at the same time.
translations.idiomatic: When the heir fell, they perished with him on the same day.

sentences.zh: 衛后、史良悌葬長安城南。

translations.literal: Empress Wei and Lady Shi the worthy concubine were buried south of Chang'an city.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Wei and Consort Shi were interred south of Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 史皇孫、皇孫妃王夫人及皇女孫葬廣明。

translations.literal: The imperial grandson of the Shi line, the imperial grandson's consort Lady Wang, and the imperial granddaughter were buried at Guangming.
translations.idiomatic: The imperial grandson of the Shi line, his consort Lady Wang, and their daughter were buried at Guangming.

sentences.zh: 皇孫二人隨太子者，與太子並葬湖。

translations.literal: The two imperial grandsons who followed the crown prince, with the crown prince together were buried at Hu.
translations.idiomatic: The two grandsons who had fled with the crown prince were laid to rest beside him at Hu.

sentences.zh: 後八歲，有司復言：「《禮》『父為士，子為天子，祭以天子』。

translations.literal: After eight years, the offices again said: 'According to the Rites, "When the father is a knight and the son is Son of Heaven, sacrifice with Son of Heaven."'
translations.idiomatic: Eight years later, officials reported: 'The Rites say, "When the father holds only a knight's rank and the son becomes Son of Heaven, the son sacrifices to him with Son of Heaven's rites."'

sentences.zh: 悼園宜稱尊號曰皇考，立廟，因園為寢，以時薦享焉。

translations.literal: The Daoyuan park should receive honored title saying Imperial Father, establish a temple, take the park as inner shrine, and thereby seasonally offer and feast.'
translations.idiomatic: The memorial park at Daoyuan should be titled Imperial Father; build a temple there, use the park as the inner shrine, and offer the seasonal sacrifices.'

sentences.zh: 益奉園民滿千六百家，以為奉明縣。

translations.literal: Increase the households serving the park until full one thousand six hundred families, and make them Fengming County.'
translations.idiomatic: Raise the tomb-guard households to sixteen hundred families and organize their lands as Fengming County.'

sentences.zh: 尊戾夫人曰戾后，置園奉邑，及益戾園各滿三百家。」

translations.literal: Honor Lady Li as Empress Li, establish park and fief for offering, and increase each Li park to three hundred households each.'
translations.idiomatic: Posthumously honor Lady Li as Empress Li, endow her with cemetery fiefs, and bring each of the Li heirs' memorial grounds up to three hundred maintaining households each.'

sentences.zh: 齊懷王閎

translations.literal: Prince Huai of Qi, Hong.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Hong, Prince Huai of Qi.

sentences.zh: 燕刺王旦

translations.literal: Prince Ci of Yan, Dan.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Dan, Prince of Yan (posthumously styled Ci, 'the Pricked').

sentences.zh: 燕刺王旦賜策曰：「嗚呼！

translations.literal: To Prince Ci of Yan, Dan, the enfeoffment document said: 'Alas!'
translations.idiomatic: The edict investing Prince Dan of Yan began: 'Alas!'

sentences.zh: 小子旦，受茲玄社，建爾國家，封於北土，世為漢籓輔。

translations.literal: Young son Dan, receive this dark earth, establish your state and family, enfeoff on the northern soil, generation to generation be Han fence and screen.'
translations.idiomatic: 'Young Dan, receive this sacred black soil, found your kingdom on the northern marches, and for age after age stand as bulwark and shield for the house of Han.'

sentences.zh: 嗚呼！

translations.literal: Alas!'
translations.idiomatic: 'Alas!'

sentences.zh: 薰鬻氏虐老獸心，以奸巧邊甿。

translations.literal: The Xunyu clan cruel-old beast heart, with treachery and trickery harm the border commoners.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu show the hearts of beasts: they abandon the aged to misery and harry our border folk with every deceit they know.

sentences.zh: 朕命將率，租征厥罪。

translations.literal: We command the generals and leaders, rent and march to punish their crimes.
translations.idiomatic: We have ordered our generals to march and exact retribution for their crimes.

sentences.zh: 萬夫長、千夫長，三十有二帥，降旗奔師。

translations.literal: Ten-thousand-husband chiefs, thousand-husband chiefs, thirty-two in all leading, lowered banners and fled the army.
translations.idiomatic: Thirty-two commanders of ten-thousand and thousand-man units lowered their banners and broke before our host.

sentences.zh: 薰鬻徙域，北州以妥。

translations.literal: The Xunyu shifted territory; the northern province thereby was settled.
translations.idiomatic: The nomads have been driven beyond the pale, and the north is quiet at last.

sentences.zh: 悉爾心，毋作怨，毋作棐德，毋乃廢備。

translations.literal: Exhaust your heart, do not nurture resentment, do not nurture wicked virtue, do not then abolish readiness.
translations.idiomatic: Give the realm your whole heart: nurse no private grudges, foster no wicked ways, and never relax your guard.

sentences.zh: 非教士不得從征。

translations.literal: Non-instructed knights may not follow the expedition.
translations.idiomatic: Only trained soldiers may ride with the hosts We despatch.

sentences.zh: 王其戒之！」

translations.literal: O King, guard against this!'
translations.idiomatic: Take these words to heart, O king!'

sentences.zh: 帝崩，太子立，是為孝昭帝，賜諸侯王璽書。

translations.literal: The Emperor died; the crown prince was established; this is Emperor Xiaozhao; he granted sealed edicts to the feudal kings.
translations.idiomatic: When the old emperor died, the crown prince ascended as Emperor Zhao and sent sealed rescripts to the kings of the blood.

sentences.zh: 旦得書，不肯哭，曰：「璽書封小。

translations.literal: When Dan obtained the writing, he refused to weep, saying: 'The seal on the rescript is small.'
translations.idiomatic: Dan read his copy and refused to mourn, muttering, 'The wax on this rescript is too small.'

sentences.zh: 京師疑有變。」

translations.literal: The capital suspects there is a change.'
translations.idiomatic: 'Something must be wrong in the capital.'

sentences.zh: 遣幸臣壽西長、孫縱之、王孺等之長安，以問禮儀為名。

translations.literal: He sent favored ministers Shouxi Zhang, Sun Zongzhi, Wang Ru, and others to Chang'an, nominally to ask about ritual propriety.
translations.idiomatic: He dispatched his favorites—Shouxi Zhang, Sun Zongzhi, Wang Ru, and others—to Chang'an under the pretense of inquiring about mourning etiquette.

sentences.zh: 王孺見執金吾廣意，問：「帝崩所病？

translations.literal: Wang Ru saw Chief of the Metropolitan Guards Guangyi, and asked: 'By what illness did the Emperor die?'
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ru called on Guangyi, chief of the metropolitan guard, and asked, 'What illness carried off the emperor?'

sentences.zh: 立者誰子？

translations.literal: The one established—whose son?
translations.idiomatic: Who is the new heir?'

sentences.zh: 年幾歲？」

translations.literal: How many years old?'
translations.idiomatic: How old is he?'

sentences.zh: 廣意言：「待詔五莋宮，宮中言雚言帝崩，諸將軍共立太子為帝，年八九歲，葬時不出臨。」

translations.literal: Guangyi said: 'While awaiting edict at Wuzuo Palace, inside the palace there was clamor saying the Emperor died; the various generals together established the crown prince as Emperor; he is eight or nine years; at burial time he did not go out to attend.'
translations.idiomatic: Guangyi replied: 'I was on duty at Wuzuo Palace when word raced through the halls that the emperor was dead. The marshals enthroned the crown prince—a boy of eight or nine—and he never even appeared at the funeral.'

sentences.zh: 歸以報王。

translations.literal: Returning, he reported to the King.
translations.idiomatic: They rode home and told the prince everything.

sentences.zh: 王曰：「上棄群臣，無語言，蓋主又不得見，甚可怪也。」

translations.literal: The King said: 'The Superior cast off the assembled ministers, without words; the Princess of Gai again could not obtain audience; very strange.'
translations.idiomatic: Dan said, 'The late emperor dismissed his ministers without a word, and even the Princess of Gai was barred from his bedside—something is very wrong here.'

sentences.zh: 復遣中大夫至京師上書言：「竊見孝武皇帝躬聖道，孝宗廟，慈愛骨肉，和集兆民，德配天地，明並日月，威武洋溢，遠方執寶而朝，增郡數十，斥地且倍，封泰山，禪梁父，巡狩天下，遠方珍物陳於太廟，德甚休盛，請立廟郡國。」

translations.literal: Again he sent Grandee of the Palace to the capital to submit a memorial saying: 'Your servant has seen how Emperor Xiaowu personally embodied the sage way, was filial to the ancestral temple, was compassionate to flesh and bone, harmonized the hundred surnames, virtue matched Heaven and Earth, brightness paired sun and moon, martial might overflowed, distant regions held treasure and came to court, increased commanderies by several tens, expanded territory nearly doubled, enfeoffed Mount Tai, knelt Liangfu, toured and inspected all under Heaven, distant regions' precious things were displayed in the Grand Temple; virtue was very beautiful and great; please establish temples in commanderies and kingdoms.'
translations.idiomatic: He next sent a palace grandee to the capital with a memorial: 'Your servant has witnessed how Emperor Wu embraced the Way of the sages, honored his ancestors, cherished his kin, and brought peace to the common people. His virtue matched heaven and earth, his clarity rivaled the sun and moon, and his might rolled outward until distant peoples offered tribute. He added dozens of commanderies, doubled the realm's breadth, sacrificed on Mount Tai, knelt on Liangfu, toured the four quarters, and laid the rarest treasures before the Grand Temple. Merit so towering calls for shrines in every province—we ask that temples to him be raised throughout the commanderies and kingdoms.'

sentences.zh: 奏報聞。

translations.literal: The memorial was submitted and heard.
translations.idiomatic: The court received the memorial and filed it without reply.

sentences.zh: 時大將軍霍光秉政，褒賜燕王錢三千萬，益封萬三千戶。

translations.literal: At the time Grand General Huo Guang held government; he praised and granted the Prince of Yan thirty million cash, increased enfeoffment ten thousand three thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Marshal Huo Guang was running the government; to placate the king of Yan he awarded him thirty million cash and added thirteen thousand households to his fief.

sentences.zh: 旦怒曰：「我當為帝，何賜也！」

translations.literal: Dan angrily said: 'I ought to have been Emperor—what granting is this!'
translations.idiomatic: Dan snarled, 'The throne should have been mine—what insult is this, tossing me money?'

sentences.zh: 遂與宗室中山哀王子劉長、齊孝王孫劉澤等結謀，詐言以武帝時受詔，得職吏事，修武備，備非常。

translations.literal: Then he with imperial-clan Prince Ai of Zhongshan's son Liu Chang, Prince Xiao of Qi's grandson Liu Ze, and others knotted a plot, falsely saying that in Emperor Wu's time they had received an edict, obtained charge of clerical affairs, repaired martial readiness, and prepared for the extraordinary.
translations.idiomatic: He then conspired with Liu Chang, son of the prince of Zhongshan, Liu Ze, grandson of Prince Xiao of Qi, and others, claiming they had been secretly authorized under Emperor Wu to oversee administration, drill troops, and stand ready for any emergency.

sentences.zh: 長於是為旦命令群臣曰：「寡人賴先帝休德，獲奉北籓，親受明詔，職吏事，領庫兵，飭武備，任重職大，夙夜兢兢，子大夫將何以規佐寡人？

translations.literal: Liu Chang thereupon for Dan ordered the assembled ministers, saying: 'I, the unworthy one, rely on the late Emperor's beautiful virtue, have obtained to hold the northern fence, personally received the clear edict, hold charge of clerical affairs, lead storehouse weapons, set in order martial readiness; the burden is weighty, the office great; morning and night I am fearful and careful—how will you, gentlemen, plan to counsel and assist me?
translations.idiomatic: Liu Chang then had Prince Dan summon his ministers and address them: I owe my post on the northern frontier to my late fathers grace, and I hold a mandate to oversee officials, arsenals, and defenses. The burden is heavy and I lie awake with worry—tell me, gentlemen, how do you propose to advise me?

sentences.zh: 且燕國雖小，成周之建國也，上自召公，下及昭、襄，於今千載，豈可謂無賢哉？

translations.literal: Moreover although the state of Yan is small, it was a state founded in the Cheng-Zhou founding; from Duke of Shao above down to Zhao and Xiang of Qin, down to now a thousand years—can one say there are no worthies?
translations.idiomatic: Yan may be a small kingdom, yet it was founded in the age of King Cheng of Zhou—from the Duke of Shao down through the Zhao and Xiang reigns of Qin, a thousand years have passed. Surely it cannot be said that no worthy men have ever walked this land?

sentences.zh: 寡人束帶聽朝三十餘年，曾無聞焉。

translations.literal: I, the unworthy one, have belted myself and listened to court thirty-odd years, and never once heard of them.
translations.idiomatic: For more than thirty years I have risen at dawn and held court, yet I have never heard a whisper of such talent.

sentences.zh: 其者寡人之不及與？

translations.literal: Perhaps it is my unworthiness?
translations.idiomatic: Is the fault mine—that I am not equal to the task?

sentences.zh: 意亦子大夫之思有所不至乎？

translations.literal: Or is it also that you gentlemen's thoughts do not reach far enough?
translations.idiomatic: Or is it that you, my officers, have simply not troubled to think the matter through?

sentences.zh: 其咎安在？

translations.literal: Where does the blame lie?
translations.idiomatic: Where, then, does the blame lie?

sentences.zh: 方今寡人欲撟邪防非，章聞揚和，撫慰百姓，移風易俗，厥路何由？

translations.literal: Now I, the unworthy one, desire to straighten the crooked and guard against wrong, make heard and raise harmony, soothe and comfort the hundred surnames, shift customs and change practices—by what road may this be done?
translations.idiomatic: I mean to set crooked things straight, bar the door to evil, give the good a voice, bring peace to the people, and reform the ways of the land—by what path can that be done?

sentences.zh: 子大夫其各悉心以對，寡人將察焉。」

translations.literal: You gentlemen, each exhaust your minds to answer; I, the unworthy one, will examine it.'
translations.idiomatic: Each of you, gentlemen, give me your best counsel in full; I will weigh every word.'

sentences.zh: 群臣皆免冠謝。

translations.literal: The assembled ministers all removed caps and apologized.
translations.idiomatic: The whole assembly doffed their caps and begged forgiveness in silence.

sentences.zh: 郎中成軫謂旦曰：「大王失職，獨可起而索，不可坐而得也。

translations.literal: Gentleman-of-the-household Cheng Zhen said to Dan: 'The Great King has lost his office; you can only rise and seek it—you cannot obtain it by sitting.'
translations.idiomatic: Cheng Zhen of the palace guard whispered to Dan: You have been robbed of your rightful place; you must seize it by force—you will never win it by sitting still.

sentences.zh: 大王一起，國中雖女子皆奮臂隨大王。」

translations.literal: When the Great King rises, though there be women in the state, all will raise arms and follow the Great King.'
translations.idiomatic: Raise your standard, and every soul in Yan, even the women, will take up arms at your side.'

sentences.zh: 旦曰：「前高後時，偽立子弘為皇帝，諸侯交手事之八年。

translations.literal: Dan said: 'In former times under Empress Gao, they falsely set up Prince Hong as emperor; the feudal lords with crossed hands served him eight years.'
translations.idiomatic: Dan replied: Remember the Empress Dowager Lü and her puppet Hong—the lords bowed their heads and served that sham emperor for eight years.

sentences.zh: 呂太后崩，大臣誅諸呂，迎立文帝，天下乃知非孝惠子也。

translations.literal: When Empress Dowager Lü died, the great ministers executed the various Lüs, welcomed and established Emperor Wen, and then all under Heaven knew he was not Emperor Xiaohui's son.'
translations.idiomatic: When she died, the great ministers wiped out the Lü clan and enthroned Emperor Wen—and only then did the realm learn that the boy on the throne had never been Xiaohui's true son.'

sentences.zh: 我親武帝長子，反不得立，上書請立廟，又不聽。

translations.literal: I am Emperor Wu's eldest son by blood, yet on the contrary was not established; I submitted a memorial asking to establish temples, and again was not heeded.'
translations.idiomatic: I am Emperor Wu's firstborn son, yet I was passed over. I asked only to honor him with temples, and the court would not even hear me out.'

sentences.zh: 立者疑非劉氏。」

translations.literal: The one established is suspected not to be of the Liu surname.'
translations.idiomatic: Men whisper that the child they enthroned may not be a Liu at all.'

sentences.zh: 即與劉澤謀為奸書，言少帝非武帝子，大臣所共立，天下宜共伐之。

translations.literal: Immediately he with Liu Ze plotted to make a treasonous document, saying the young emperor was not Emperor Wu's son, that the great ministers together established him, and all under Heaven ought together to attack him.
translations.idiomatic: He and Liu Ze forged a seditious proclamation claiming the boy-emperor was no son of Emperor Wu, that the high ministers had installed him by conspiracy, and that the empire should rise as one to destroy him.

sentences.zh: 使人傳行郡國，以搖動百姓。

translations.literal: They sent men to circulate it through commanderies and kingdoms, thereby to shake and move the hundred surnames.
translations.idiomatic: Agents carried the forgery from kingdom to kingdom, hoping to stir the common people into revolt.

sentences.zh: 澤謀歸發兵臨淄，與燕王俱起。

translations.literal: Ze plotted to return and raise troops at Linzi, together with the Prince of Yan both rising.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Ze planned to slip back to Linzi, raise an army there, and move in concert with the king of Yan.

sentences.zh: 旦遂招來郡國奸人，賦斂銅鐵作甲兵，數閱其車騎材官卒，建旌旗鼓車，旄頭先驅，郎中侍從者著貂羽，黃金附蟬，皆號侍中。

translations.literal: Dan then summoned and attracted commanderies' and kingdoms' wicked men, levied copper and iron to make armour and weapons, repeatedly reviewed his chariots, cavalry, skilled soldiers, and foot soldiers, set up banners, drums, and drum-carts, yak-tail pennons going before; gentlemen-of-the-household and attendants wore sable tails, gold with cicada ornaments, all styled themselves palace attendants.
translations.idiomatic: Dan began recruiting ruffians from across the north, melting down copper and iron for arms, drilling charioteers, cavalry, and crossbowmen, and parading war drums and yak-tailed standards through the streets. His household guards pinned sable plumes and gold cicadas to their caps and swaggered about calling themselves imperial attendants.

sentences.zh: 旦從相、中尉以下，勒車騎，發民會圍，大獵文安縣，以講士馬，須期日。

translations.literal: Dan from the chancellor and central commandant down, pressed chariots and cavalry, issued the people to assemble for a hunt, greatly hunted in Wen'an County, thereby to drill knights and horses, awaiting the appointed day.
translations.idiomatic: From his chancellor and chief of police on down, he mustered chariots and horsemen, called out the yeomanry for a great hunt in Wen'an County, and used the exercise as a cover to train troops for the day he would strike.

sentences.zh: 郎中韓義等數諫旦，旦殺義等凡十五人。

translations.literal: Gentlemen-of-the-household Han Yi and others repeatedly remonstrated Dan; Dan killed Yi and others in all fifteen persons.
translations.idiomatic: When courtiers such as Han Yi dared remonstrate, Dan cut them down—fifteen in all.

sentences.zh: 會缾侯劉成知澤等謀，告之青州刺史雋不疑，不疑收捕澤以聞。

translations.literal: It happened that Marquis of Ping Liu Cheng learned of Ze and others' plot, reported it to Qing Province Governor Jun Buyi; Buyi seized and captured Ze and reported it.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis of Ping Liu Cheng got wind of the plot and denounced it to Jun Buyi, governor of Qing province, who arrested Liu Ze and forwarded the case to the throne.

sentences.zh: 天子遣大鴻臚丞治，連引燕王。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven sent the Grand Herald's assistant to try the case, continuously implicating the Prince of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor ordered an assistant grand herald to conduct the inquiry, and the evidence soon pointed toward the king of Yan.

sentences.zh: 有詔勿治，而劉澤等伏誅。

translations.literal: There was an edict not to try him, but Liu Ze and others submitted to execution.
translations.idiomatic: An edict spared the king of Yan himself, but Liu Ze and his fellow plotters went to the block.

sentences.zh: 益封缾侯。

translations.literal: Increase enfeoffment to Marquis of Ping.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Cheng was rewarded with an enlarged fief for his loyalty.

sentences.zh: 久之，旦姊鄂邑蓋長公主、左將軍上官桀父子與霍光爭權有隙，皆知旦怨光，即私與燕交通。

translations.literal: After a long time, Dan's elder sister the Princess of Eyi, Elder of Gai, Grand General of the Left Shangguan Jie and his son, contending power with Huo Guang had a rift; all knew Dan resented Guang, and therefore privately communicated with Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Years later, Dans sister—the princess of Eyi, styled Elder of Gai—and the father-and-son team of Shangguan Jie, general of the left, fell out with Huo Guang over power. They knew Dan nursed a grudge against Guang, and secret messengers began passing between the palace and Yan.

sentences.zh: 旦遣孫縱之等前後十餘輩，多繼金寶走馬，賂遺蓋主。

translations.literal: Dan sent Sun Zongzhi and others, in all over ten parties, mostly carrying gold and treasure, swift horses, bribing and presenting to the Elder of Gai.
translations.idiomatic: Dan dispatched Sun Zongzhi and a dozen other envoys, laden with gold, jewels, and fleet horses, to buy the princess of Gai's favor.

sentences.zh: 上官桀及御史大夫桑弘羊等皆與交通，數記疏光過失與旦，令上書告之。

translations.literal: Shangguan Jie and Imperial Counselor Sang Hongyang and others all communicated with them, repeatedly recording and writing out Guang's faults for Dan, ordering him to submit memorials reporting them.
translations.idiomatic: Shangguan Jie, Sang Hongyang the imperial counselor, and their faction joined the intrigue, jotting down every real or imagined slip by Huo Guang for Dan to weave into memorials of impeachment.

sentences.zh: 桀欲從中下其章。

translations.literal: Jie wished to have the memorial from the center approve his document.
translations.idiomatic: Jie hoped to smuggle the memorial through the inner palace so that the emperor would endorse it without scrutiny.

sentences.zh: 旦聞之，喜，上疏曰：「昔秦據南面之位，制一世之命，威服四夷，輕弱骨肉，顯重異族，廢道任刑，無恩宗室。

translations.literal: When Dan heard it, he was pleased, and submitted a memorial saying: 'In olden times Qin occupied the south-facing position, controlled one generation's commands, awe-subdued the four barbarians, belittled and weakened flesh and bone, displayed weight to different surnames, abandoned the Way and employed punishments, without grace to the imperial clan.'
translations.idiomatic: Dan was delighted and forwarded a long memorial: In antiquity Qin faced south as Son of Heaven, bent the age to its will, cowed the four quarters, humbled its own kin while lavishing favor on outsiders, cast aside the Way for the lash, and showed no kindness to the house of its blood.'

sentences.zh: 其後尉佗入南夷，陳涉呼楚澤，近狎作亂，內外俱發，趙氏無炊火焉。

translations.literal: Afterwards Wei Tuo entered the southern barbarians, Chen She cried out in the marshes of Chu, near and familiar ones made rebellion, inner and outer together arose, and the Zhao house had no cooking-fire.
translations.idiomatic: Then Zhao Tuo carved up the south, Chen She raised the cry of revolt in the Chu marshes, trusted men turned traitor within and without, and the First Emperor's line was snuffed out like a cold stove.'

sentences.zh: 高皇帝覽蹤跡，觀得失，見秦建本非是，故改其路，規土連城，布王子孫，是以支葉扶疏，異姓不得間也。

translations.literal: The August Emperor traced tracks, viewed gain and loss, saw that Qin's founding the root was not right, therefore changed its road, planned earth and linked cities, spread out kings and grandsons, therefore branches and leaves were luxuriant, different surnames could not insert themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Our own Gaozu studied those ruins, saw where Qin had gone wrong, and rebuilt the realm on a different plan: he enfeoffed his sons and grandsons so that the dynastic tree grew thick with branches, leaving no crevice for outsiders to slip in.'

sentences.zh: 今陛下承明繼成，委任公卿，群臣連與成朋，非毀宗室，膚受之訴，日騁於廷，惡吏廢法立威，主恩不及下究。

translations.literal: Now Your Majesty inherits brightness and continues completion, entrusts office to dukes and ministers; the assembled ministers join and form cliques, together slander the imperial house; skin-receiving complaints daily gallop in the court; evil officials abolish law and establish awe; the lord's grace does not reach down to exhaust.
translations.idiomatic: Yet today, though Your Majesty sits where he sat, power rests with grandees who pack the court in factions, blacken the Liu clan with rumor, and let petty suits shouted in the audience hall overturn the law. Cruel clerks swagger while true grace never reaches the common people.'

sentences.zh: 臣聞武帝使中郎將蘇武使匈奴，見留二十年不降，還但為典屬國。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard Emperor Wu sent Palace Gentleman Su Wu as envoy to the Xiongnu; he was detained twenty years and did not submit; on returning he was only made Superintendent of Dependent States.
translations.idiomatic: Your servant recalls how Su Wu spent twenty years a captive among the Xiongnu without yielding, and on his return was given nothing higher than superintendent of dependent states.'

sentences.zh: 今大將軍長史敞無勞，為搜粟都尉。

translations.literal: Now the grand general's chief clerk Chang has no merit, yet is made Commissioner for the Granaries.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile Huo Guang's chief clerk Yang Chang, a man who never drew sword in the state's service, walks off with the granaries commission.'

sentences.zh: 又將軍都郎羽林，道上移蹕，太官先置。

translations.literal: Again the general reviews the gentlemen at the Feathered Forest; on the road the imperial train is shifted; the Grand Provisioner sets out first.
translations.idiomatic: He reviews the palace guard as if it were his private army, clears the highways for his own retinue, and sends the imperial kitchen ahead to lay his table before the emperor's.'

sentences.zh: 臣旦願歸符璽，入宿衛，察奸臣之變。」

translations.literal: Your servant Dan wishes to return tallies and seals, enter and perform night guard, and investigate wicked ministers' changes.'
translations.idiomatic: I beg leave to surrender my seals, take a turn standing night watch in the capital myself, and help root out the traitors who surround the throne.'

sentences.zh: 是時，昭帝年十四，覺其有詐，遂親信霍光，而疏上官桀等。

translations.literal: At that time Emperor Zhao was fourteen years old, perceived there was fraud, then personally trusted Huo Guang, and kept Shangguan Jie and others at distance.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhao was only fourteen, but he smelled a rat. He drew closer to Huo Guang and cold-shouldered Shangguan Jie and his friends.

sentences.zh: 桀等因謀共殺光，廢帝，迎立燕王為天子。

translations.literal: Jie and others therefore plotted together to kill Guang, depose the Emperor, welcome and establish the Prince of Yan as Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: The cabal then resolved to murder Huo Guang, depose the young emperor, and enthrone the king of Yan in his stead.

sentences.zh: 旦置驛書，往來相報，許立桀為王，外連郡國豪傑以千數。

translations.literal: Dan set up post-station letters, going and coming mutually reporting, promised to establish Jie as king, outwardly linked commanderies' and kingdoms' stalwarts by the thousands.
translations.idiomatic: Dan relayed orders by post riders, promised Shangguan Jie a kingdom of his own, and boasted of thousands of bravos in the provinces ready to rise at his word.

sentences.zh: 旦以語相平，平曰：「大王前與劉澤結謀，事未成而發覺者，以劉澤素誇，好侵陵也。

translations.literal: Dan spoke of it to Chancellor Ping; Ping said: 'The Great King before with Liu Ze knotted a plot; the affair did not succeed but was discovered, because Liu Ze by nature brags and likes to invade and bully.'
translations.idiomatic: Dan confided in his chancellor Ping, who answered: Your last scheme with Liu Ze failed because Liu Ze could not keep his mouth shut—he strutted and bullied until someone talked.'

sentences.zh: 平聞左將軍素輕易，車騎將軍少而驕，臣恐其如劉澤時不能成，又恐既成，反大王也。」

translations.literal: Ping has heard the General of the Left is by nature light and easy, the General of Chariots and Cavalry is young and arrogant; your servant fears they will be like Liu Ze's time and cannot succeed, and also fears that if they succeed, they will turn against the Great King.'
translations.idiomatic: The general of the left is rash by nature, and the general of chariots and cavalry is young and proud. I fear they will bungle the business as Liu Ze did—or worse, if they succeed, they will turn the knife on you.'

sentences.zh: 旦曰：「前日一男子詣闕，自謂故太子，長安中民趣鄉之，正言雚不可止，大將軍恐，出兵陳之，以自備耳。

translations.literal: Dan said: 'The other day one man went to the palace gates, calling himself the former crown prince; the people of Chang'an hurried to him; straight speech clamor could not be stopped; the Grand General was afraid, issued troops and arrayed them, for self-readiness only.'
translations.idiomatic: Dan snorted: A few years ago a beggar presented himself at the Weiyang gates claiming to be the late crown prince, and half of Chang'an ran to cheer him until Huo Guang panicked and threw troops across the avenues—for all the good it did him.'

sentences.zh: 我帝長子，天下所信，何憂見反？」

translations.literal: I am the Emperor's eldest son, what all under Heaven trusts—why worry about being turned against?'
translations.idiomatic: I am the late emperor's eldest son and the man the realm still trusts. Who would dare turn on me?'

sentences.zh: 後謂群臣：「蓋主報言，獨患大將軍與右將軍王莽。

translations.literal: Afterwards he told the assembled ministers: 'The Elder of Gai reports, the sole worry is the Grand General and the General of the Right Wang Mang.'
translations.idiomatic: Later he told his courtiers: Word from the princess of Gai is that only Huo Guang and Wang Mang, the general of the right, stand in our way.'

sentences.zh: 今右將軍物故，丞相病，幸事必成，征不久。」

translations.literal: Now the General of the Right has died, the prime minister is ill; fortunately the affair is sure to succeed; the summons will not be long.'
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang is dead, the chancellor is bedridden—Heaven is clearing our path. The summons to strike cannot be long in coming.'

sentences.zh: 令群臣皆裝。

translations.literal: He ordered the assembled ministers all to prepare dress.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered every man in his train to prepare traveling gear.

sentences.zh: 是時天雨，虹下屬宮中飲井水，井水竭。

translations.literal: At that time heaven rained; a rainbow descended and attached inside the palace to the well water; the well water dried up.
translations.idiomatic: That season the skies opened; a rainbow dipped into the palace well until the water ran dry.

sentences.zh: 廁中豕群出，壞大官灶。

translations.literal: In the privy a herd of pigs came out, broke the Grand Provisioner's stove.
translations.idiomatic: Pigs burst from the palace privy and smashed the imperial kitchen hearth.

sentences.zh: 烏鵲斗死。

translations.literal: Crows and magpies fought to the death.
translations.idiomatic: Crows and magpies tore each other dead on the rooftiles.

sentences.zh: 鼠舞殿端門中。

translations.literal: Rats danced in the hall's end gate.
translations.idiomatic: Rats danced in the lintel of the main hall.

sentences.zh: 殿上戶自閉，不可開。

translations.literal: The hall's doors closed of themselves and could not be opened.
translations.idiomatic: The great doors of the audience hall slammed shut of their own weight and could not be forced.

sentences.zh: 天火燒城門。

translations.literal: Heaven's fire burned the city gate.
translations.idiomatic: Lightning struck the city gate.

sentences.zh: 大風壞宮城樓，折拔樹木。

translations.literal: Great wind broke the palace city tower, snapped and uprooted trees.
translations.idiomatic: A gale shattered the watchtower on the palace wall and tore up trees by the roots.

sentences.zh: 流星下墮。

translations.literal: A shooting star fell.
translations.idiomatic: A boulder-bright meteor fell across the sky.

sentences.zh: 後姬以下皆恐。

translations.literal: The secondary consorts down were all frightened.
translations.idiomatic: Every concubine in the harem trembled with fear.

sentences.zh: 王驚病，使人祠葭水、台水。

translations.literal: The King was alarmed and ill, sent men to sacrifice at Jia River and Tai River.
translations.idiomatic: The king fell sick with dread and sent priests to offer sacrifice at the Jia and Tai rivers.

sentences.zh: 王客呂廣等知星，為王言「當有兵圍城，期在九月、十月，漢當有大臣戮死者」。

translations.literal: The King's retainers Lü Guang and others knew the stars; for the King they said: 'There ought to be troops besieging the city; the period falls in the ninth and tenth months; Han ought to have a great minister executed by death.'
translations.idiomatic: His star-readers, men like Lü Guang, warned him: Armies will ring the walls in the ninth or tenth month, and a great minister of Han will die by the headsman's axe.'

sentences.zh: 語具在《五行志》。

translations.literal: The words are fully in the 'Treatise on the Five Phases.'
translations.idiomatic: The full portents are recorded in the Treatise on the Five Phases.

sentences.zh: 王愈憂恐，謂廣等曰：「謀事不成，妖祥數見，兵氣且至，奈何？」

translations.literal: The King grew more anxious and fearful, said to Guang and others: 'The plot does not succeed, prodigies repeatedly appear, martial vapor is about to arrive—what is to be done?'
translations.idiomatic: Dan grew frantic. He asked his soothsayers: Our plot stalls while omens pile up and the air itself reeks of steel—what are we to do?'

sentences.zh: 會蓋主舍人父燕倉知其謀，告之，由是發覺。

translations.literal: It happened that the Elder of Gai's chamberlain's father Yan Cang knew their plot, reported it; by this it was discovered.
translations.idiomatic: Then Yan Cang, father of one of the princess of Gai's chamberlains, betrayed the conspiracy to the authorities.

sentences.zh: 丞相賜璽書，部中二千石逐捕孫縱之及左將軍桀等，皆伏誅。

translations.literal: The prime minister granted a sealed edict; the office's two-thousand-bushel ranks pursued and captured Sun Zongzhi and the General of the Left Jie and others; all submitted to execution.
translations.idiomatic: The chancellor issued sealed orders, and men of two-thousand-bushel rank hunted down Sun Zongzhi, Shangguan Jie, and their confederates. Every one of them died on the scaffold.

sentences.zh: 旦聞之，召相平曰：「事敗，遂發兵乎？」

translations.literal: When Dan heard it, he summoned Chancellor Ping, saying: 'The affair is defeated—shall we then issue troops?'
translations.idiomatic: When the news reached Yan, Dan called for Ping and demanded: The plot is blown—do we strike now?'

sentences.zh: 平曰：「左將軍已死，百姓皆知之，不可發也。」

translations.literal: Ping said: 'The General of the Left is already dead; the hundred surnames all know it; troops cannot be issued.'
translations.idiomatic: Ping answered: Shangguan Jie is dead and the whole empire knows it. There will be no rising from this ashes.'

sentences.zh: 王憂懣，置酒萬載宮，會賓客、群臣、妃妾坐飲。

translations.literal: The King, anxious and depressed, set out ale at Wansui Palace, assembled guests, assembled ministers, concubines and secondary consorts, sat and drank.
translations.idiomatic: Broken with grief, Dan called a last banquet in Wansui Palace for his ministers, guests, and women.

sentences.zh: 王自歌曰：「歸空城兮，狗不吠，雞不鳴，橫術何廣廣兮，固知國中之無人！」

translations.literal: The King himself sang, saying: 'Return to the empty city—dogs do not bark, chickens do not crow; how broad and wide the crossroads—solidly I know there is no person in the state!'
translations.idiomatic: He lifted his voice and sang: Back to an empty city—no dog barks, no cock crows; how wide the crossroads run—yet I know now my kingdom holds no friend at all!'

sentences.zh: 華容夫人起舞曰：「發紛紛兮寘渠，骨籍籍兮亡居。

translations.literal: Lady Huarong rose and danced, saying: 'Hair disheveled—place in the ditch; bones piled—no dwelling.'
translations.idiomatic: His favorite, Lady Huarong, rose and danced, chanting: Locks tangled, tossed in the ditch; bones piled high, with never a roof over them.'

sentences.zh: 母求死子兮，妻求死夫。

translations.literal: Mothers seek dead sons—wives seek dead husbands.'
translations.idiomatic: Mothers hunt for sons already slain; wives hunt for husbands already slain.'

sentences.zh: 裴回兩渠間兮，君子獨安居！」

translations.literal: Hesitating between the two ditches—where does the gentleman alone peacefully dwell!'
translations.idiomatic: They wander between twin ditches—while you, my lord, think still to find a peaceful bed!'

sentences.zh: 坐者皆泣。

translations.literal: Those seated all wept.
translations.idiomatic: Every guest at the table wept aloud.

sentences.zh: 有赦令到，王讀之，曰：「嗟乎！

translations.literal: There was an amnesty order arrived; the King read it, saying: 'Alas!'
translations.idiomatic: An imperial messenger arrived with an act of grace. Dan read it and cried: Ah—'

sentences.zh: 獨赦吏民，不赦我。」

translations.literal: It only amnesties clerks and people, it does not amnesty me.'
translations.idiomatic: It pardons every clerk and commoner in the land, but not me.'

sentences.zh: 因迎後姬諸夫人之明光殿，王曰：「老虜曹為事當族！」

translations.literal: Therefore he welcomed the secondary consorts and various ladies to Mingguang Hall; the King said: 'Old captives when they do affairs ought to be exterminated by clan!'
translations.idiomatic: He gathered his women in Mingguang Hall and raged: Those gray-bearded schemers in Chang'an deserve the extermination of their whole clans!'

sentences.zh: 欲自殺。

translations.literal: He wished to kill himself.
translations.idiomatic: He reached for his sword to fall on it.

sentences.zh: 左右曰：「黨得削國，幸不死。」

translations.literal: Those at his side said: 'If by luck the state is reduced, fortunately you will not die.'
translations.idiomatic: His attendants caught his arm: At worst they will shrink your fief—you may yet keep your life.'

sentences.zh: 後姬夫人共啼泣止王。

translations.literal: The secondary consorts and ladies together wept and stopped the King.
translations.idiomatic: His concubines clung to him, weeping, until he lowered the blade.

sentences.zh: 會天子使使者賜燕王璽書曰：「昔高皇帝王天下，建立子弟以籓屏社稷。

translations.literal: It happened the Son of Heaven sent an envoy granting the Prince of Yan a sealed edict, saying: 'In olden times the August Emperor kinged all under Heaven, established sons and younger brothers to fence and screen the altars of soil and grain.'
translations.idiomatic: Then an imperial rescript reached Yan: Long ago Gaozu ruled the realm and enfeoffed his sons and brothers as bulwarks of the altars of soil and grain.'

sentences.zh: 先日諸呂陰謀大逆，劉氏不絕若發，賴絳侯等誅討賊亂，尊立孝文，以安宗廟，非以中外有人，表裡相應故邪？

translations.literal: The other day the various Lüs secretly plotted great rebellion; the Liu house's not being cut off was like hair; relying on Marquis of Jiang and others they executed and punished the rebellious thieves, honored and established Emperor Xiaowen, thereby to secure the ancestral temple—not because inside and outside there were men, inner and outer mutually responding, was it so?'
translations.idiomatic: When the Lü clan conspired against the throne, the house of Liu hung by a thread until the Marquis of Jiang and his comrades struck down the traitors and raised Emperor Wen to restore the temples—did that victory come from anything but loyal hearts answering one another within and without?'

sentences.zh: 樊、酈、曹、灌，攜劍推鋒，從高皇帝墾災除害，耘鋤海內，當此之時，頭如蓬葆，勤苦至矣，然其賞不過封侯。

translations.literal: Fan, Li, Cao, and Guan, carrying swords and pushing spear-points, followed the August Emperor opening disaster and removing harm, hoeing and weeding within the seas; at this time their heads were like unkempt thistle-down, toil and suffering reached the utmost, yet their reward did not exceed being enfeoffed marquises.'
translations.idiomatic: Men like Fan Kuai, Li Shang, Cao Can, and Guan Ying marched behind Gaozu with naked blades, cleared the realm of tyrants, and went unshorn for months on end—yet the highest honor any won was a marquisate.'

sentences.zh: 今宗室子孫曾無暴衣露冠之勞，裂地而王之，分財而賜之，父死子繼，兄終弟及。

translations.literal: Now imperial-clan sons and grandsons have never had the toil of exposing clothes and bare caps; they split land and are made kings, divide wealth and are granted it; father dies son succeeds, elder brother ends younger brother inherits.'
translations.idiomatic: Their descendants today inherit kingdoms without ever having shivered in rags on a campaign field—estates fall to them unearned, treasure pours into their laps, and the succession passes smoothly from father to son or brother to brother.'

sentences.zh: 今王骨肉至親，敵吾一體，乃與他姓異族謀害社稷，親其所疏，疏其所親，有逆悖之心，無忠愛之義。

translations.literal: Now the King, flesh and bone utmost kin, matches Our one body, yet joins with other surnames and different clans to plot harm to the altars of soil and grain; you treat as intimate those you should keep distant, keep distant those you should treat as intimate; there is a heart of rebellion and perversity, without the righteousness of loyalty and love.'
translations.idiomatic: You are bone of our bone, yet you league yourself with outsiders against the house of Liu. You embrace strangers and cast off your kin; your heart is turned backward, and there is no shred of loyalty or love in it.'

sentences.zh: 如使古人有知，當何面目復奉齊酎見高祖之廟乎！」

translations.literal: If the men of old had knowledge, with what face would they again offer the autumn ale at Gaozu's temple!'
translations.idiomatic: Had the ancients eyes to see, how could you ever again lift the sacrificial cup in Gaozu's shrine?'

sentences.zh: 旦得書，以符璽屬醫工長，謝相二千石：「奉事不謹，死矣。」

translations.literal: When Dan obtained the writing, he entrusted tallies and seals to the chief medical officer, apologized to the chancellor and two-thousand-bushel ranks: 'Serving the affair was not careful—I am dead.'
translations.idiomatic: Dan read the edict, handed his seals to the court physician, and took leave of his ministers: I have served my trust badly. This is the end.'

sentences.zh: 即以綬自絞。

translations.literal: Immediately with the ribbon he hanged himself.
translations.idiomatic: Then he twisted his sash into a noose and strangled himself.

sentences.zh: 後夫人隨旦自殺者二十餘人。

translations.literal: Afterwards ladies who followed Dan in suicide were over twenty persons.
translations.idiomatic: More than twenty of his concubines followed him into death.

sentences.zh: 天子加恩，赦王太子建為庶人，賜旦謚曰刺王。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven added grace, amnestied the King's heir apparent Jian to be commoner, granted Dan posthumous title Prince Ci.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor showed mercy: the heir Jian was spared and reduced to commoner rank, while Dan was given the posthumous epithet Ci, the Pricked King.'

sentences.zh: 旦立三十八年而誅，國除。

translations.literal: Dan was established thirty-eight years and was executed; the state was removed.
translations.idiomatic: Dan had reigned thirty-eight years when his line ended and his kingdom was abolished.

sentences.zh: 後六年，宣帝即位，封旦兩子，慶為新昌侯，賢為安定侯。

translations.literal: After six years, when Emperor Xuan ascended, he enfeoffed Dan's two sons: Qing as Marquis of Xinchang, Xian as Marquis of Anding.
translations.idiomatic: Six years later Emperor Xuan enfeoffed two of Dan's surviving sons—Liu Qing as marquis of Xinchang and Liu Xian as marquis of Anding.

sentences.zh: 又立故太子建，是為廣陽頃王，二十九年薨。

translations.literal: Again he established the former heir apparent Jian; this is Prince Qing of Guangyang; twenty-nine years he died.
translations.idiomatic: He also restored the former heir Jian as Prince Qing of Guangyang, who reigned twenty-nine years before he died.

sentences.zh: 子穆王舜嗣，二十一年薨。

translations.literal: The son Prince Mu Shun succeeded; twenty-one years he died.
translations.idiomatic: His son Shun, Prince Mu of Guangyang, succeeded and ruled twenty-one years.

sentences.zh: 子思王璜嗣，二十年薨。

translations.literal: The son Prince Si Huang succeeded; twenty years he died.
translations.idiomatic: Huang, Prince Si, held the fief for twenty years and died.

sentences.zh: 子嘉嗣。

translations.literal: The son Jia succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Jia inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 王莽時，皆廢漢籓王為家人，嘉獨以獻符命封扶美侯，賜姓王氏。

translations.literal: In Wang Mang's time, all abolished Han fence kings to be common families; Jia alone because presenting tallies and commands was enfeoffed Marquis of Fumei, granted surname Wang.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Mang stripped the Han imperial clans of their kingdoms, Jia alone kept his skin by proffering bogus portents; Mang made him marquis of Fumei and forced the surname Wang on him.

sentences.zh: 廣陵厲王

translations.literal: Prince Li of Guangling.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xu, Prince Li of Guangling.

sentences.zh: 廣陵厲王胥賜策曰：「嗚呼！

translations.literal: To Prince Li of Guangling, Xu, the enfeoffment document said: 'Alas!'
translations.idiomatic: The edict investing Liu Xu, prince of Guangling, began: Alas!'

sentences.zh: 小子胥，受茲赤社，建爾國家，封於南土，世世為漢籓輔。

translations.literal: Young son Xu, receive this red earth, establish your state and family, enfeoff on the southern soil, generation to generation be Han fence and screen.'
translations.idiomatic: 'Young Xu, take this sacred red soil, found your house on the southern marches, and for all generations be shield and rampart for the Han.'

sentences.zh: 古人有言曰：『大江之南，五湖之間，其人輕心。

translations.literal: The men of old had a saying: 'South of the Great River, between the Five Lakes, its people have light hearts.'
translations.idiomatic: The ancients warned: South of the Yangzi, among the Five Lakes, the folk are quick to shift their loyalties.'

sentences.zh: 揚州保強，三代要服，不及以正。』

translations.literal: Yangzhou preserves strength; the three dynasties held it as outer service, not reaching to correct it with uprightness.'
translations.idiomatic: Yangzhou is proud and stubborn; even in the Three Dynasties it was held only as distant tributary ground, too wild to tame by the usual rites.'

sentences.zh: 嗚呼！

translations.literal: Alas!'
translations.idiomatic: Alas!'

sentences.zh: 悉爾心，祗祗兢兢，乃惠乃順，毋桐好逸，毋邇宵人，惟法惟則！

translations.literal: Exhaust your heart, reverently reverently fearful and careful, thereby be kind and thereby be compliant; do not be like the tong tree loving ease; do not draw near to night men; only law, only model!'
translations.idiomatic: Give the realm your whole heart: be humble, be vigilant, be gentle and obedient. Do not sink into idle ease like the parasol tree; do not truckle to men who haunt the dark—take the law alone as your model!'

sentences.zh: 《書》云『臣不作福，不作威』，靡有後羞。

translations.literal: The Documents says: 'Ministers do not create fortune, do not create awe'—then there will be no later shame.
translations.idiomatic: The Book of Documents warns: Ministers must not arrogate blessings or throw their weight about—only then can they escape later disgrace.'

sentences.zh: 王其戒之！」

translations.literal: O King, guard against this!'
translations.idiomatic: Take these words to heart, O king!'

sentences.zh: 胥壯大，好倡樂逸游，力扛鼎，空手搏熊彘猛獸。

translations.literal: Xu grew large and strong, loved music, wandering, and ease; his strength could shoulder a tripod; with empty hands he wrestled bears, boars, and fierce beasts.
translations.idiomatic: Xu grew into a giant who lived for music, roaming, and pleasure. He could hoist a ritual tripod and, bare-handed, grapple bears and wild boars.

sentences.zh: 動作無法度，故終不得為漢嗣。

translations.literal: His movements had no law and measure; therefore in the end he could not become Han heir.
translations.idiomatic: His conduct knew no restraint, so he was never seriously considered for the succession.

sentences.zh: 昭帝初立，益封胥萬三千戶，元鳳中入朝，復益萬戶，賜錢二千萬，黃金二千斤，安車駟馬寶劍。

translations.literal: When Emperor Zhao was first established, he increased enfeoffment to Xu by thirteen thousand households; in Yuanfeng he entered court, again was increased ten thousand households, was granted twenty million cash, two thousand jin of gold, a comfort chariot with four horses, and a precious sword.
translations.idiomatic: As soon as Emperor Zhao took the throne he added thirteen thousand households to Xu's income. During the Yuanfeng era the king visited the capital and won another ten thousand households, twenty million cash, two thousand catties of gold, a four-horse state coach, and a jeweled sword.

sentences.zh: 及宣帝即位，封胥四子聖、曾、寶、昌皆為列侯，又立胥小子弘為高密王。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xuan ascended, he enfeoffed Xu's four sons Sheng, Ceng, Bao, and Chang all as full marquises, and again established Xu's younger son Hong as King of Gaomi.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Xuan came to power he made each of Xu's four sons—Sheng, Ceng, Bao, and Chang—a full marquis, and raised the youngest, Hong, to the kingship of Gaomi.

sentences.zh: 所以褒賞甚厚。

translations.literal: Therefore praise and reward were very thick.
translations.idiomatic: The court heaped favor on him as on few other princes.

sentences.zh: 胥宮園中棗樹生十餘莖，莖正赤，葉白如素。

translations.literal: In Xu's palace garden a jujube tree grew over ten stalks; the stalks were straight red, the leaves white like raw silk.
translations.idiomatic: In the palace gardens a jujube sprouted a dozen trunks of blood-red bark and leaves pale as undyed silk.

sentences.zh: 池水變赤，魚死。

translations.literal: The pool water turned red; the fish died.
translations.idiomatic: The fishponds ran red and every fish in them floated belly-up.

sentences.zh: 有鼠晝立舞王後廷中。

translations.literal: There were rats that in daytime stood and danced in the king's rear court.
translations.idiomatic: Rats reared up in broad daylight and capered in the inner courtyard.

sentences.zh: 胥謂姬南等曰：「棗水魚鼠之怪甚可惡也。」

translations.literal: Xu said to concubine Nan and others: 'The abnormalities of jujube, water, fish, and rats are very hateful.'
translations.idiomatic: Xu muttered to Lady Nan and the rest: These omens of tree, water, fish, and rat bode ill indeed.'

sentences.zh: 居數月，祝詛事發覺，有司按驗，胥惶恐，藥殺巫及宮人二十餘人以絕口。

translations.literal: After several months, the cursing affair was discovered; the offices investigated; Xu was fearful and by poison killed the witches and over twenty palace people to stop their mouths.
translations.idiomatic: A few months later his witchcraft curses came to light. Panic-stricken, he poisoned more than twenty sorceresses and palace women to silence them.

sentences.zh: 公卿請誅胥，天子遣廷尉、大鴻臚即訊。

translations.literal: The dukes asked to execute Xu; the Son of Heaven sent the Commandant of Justice and the Grand Herald to question him on the spot.
translations.idiomatic: The high ministers demanded his head, but the emperor only sent the commandant of justice and the grand herald to take his deposition in Guangling.

sentences.zh: 胥謝曰：「罪死有餘，誠皆有之。

translations.literal: Xu apologized, saying: 'For crime death has surplus; truly all have them.'
translations.idiomatic: Xu bowed low and said: Death would not begin to pay for my guilt—I have earned every charge.'

sentences.zh: 事久遠，請歸思念具對。」

translations.literal: The affairs are long past; please allow me to return and ponder, then fully answer.'
translations.idiomatic: The old crimes are tangled in memory; let me withdraw and set my thoughts in order before I answer in full.'

sentences.zh: 胥既見使者還，置酒顯陽殿。

translations.literal: After Xu saw the envoys off on their return, he set out ale at Xianyang Hall.
translations.idiomatic: Once the envoys had started home, he held a banquet in Xianyang Hall.

sentences.zh: 召太子霸及子女董訾、胡生等夜飲，使所幸八子郭昭君、家人子趙左君等鼓瑟歌舞。

translations.literal: He summoned the heir Ba and his children Dongzi, Husheng, and others to drink at night; he ordered his beloved eighth-rank Gu Zhaojun, household women Zhao Zuojun, and others to strum the zither, sing, and dance.
translations.idiomatic: He called in his heir Liu Ba, his children Dongzi and Husheng, and the rest for a night of wine, while his favorite concubines of the eighth rank—Gu Zhaojun, Zhao Zuojun, and their companions—played the zither and danced.

sentences.zh: 王自歌曰：「欲久生兮無終，長不樂兮安窮！

translations.literal: The King himself sang, saying: 'Desiring long life—there is no end; long not happy—how can one be at the limit!'
translations.idiomatic: He lifted his cup and sang: Long life is what I crave—yet there is no end to craving; joy is what I lack—how then can this road ever end?'

sentences.zh: 奉天期兮不得須臾，千里馬兮駐待路。

translations.literal: 'Heaven's appointed term cannot wait even a moment; the thousand-li horse halts waiting on the road.'
translations.idiomatic: 'Heaven's summons brooks no delay; even a swift horse must halt when the road runs out.'

sentences.zh: 黃泉下兮幽深，人生要死，何為苦心！

translations.literal: 'Below the yellow springs it is deep and dark; human life must die—why bitterly toil the heart!'
translations.idiomatic: 'The yellow springs yawn deep and dark; every man must die—so why rack the heart with grief?'

sentences.zh: 何用為樂心所喜，出入無悰為樂亟。

translations.literal: 'What use is making merry with what the heart delights in; going out and entering without joy—making merry is hasty.'
translations.idiomatic: 'What good is pleasure if the heart finds no delight in it? To pass in and out of the halls without a spark of joy is to rush headlong through the feast of life.'

sentences.zh: 蒿裡召兮郭門閱，死不得取代庸，身自逝。」

translations.literal: 'Haoli calls—the outer gate is surveyed; in death one cannot obtain a substitute hire—the body itself passes away.'
translations.idiomatic: 'The graveyard calls beyond the city gate; no hired man can die in your place—you go down into the dark alone.'

sentences.zh: 左右悉更涕泣奏酒，至雞鳴時罷。

translations.literal: Those at his side all wept tears, presenting ale; when the cock crowed they stopped.
translations.idiomatic: His attendants wept as they poured the wine, and they did not leave off until the first cockcrow.

sentences.zh: 胥謂太子霸曰：「上遇我厚，今負之甚。

translations.literal: Xu said to heir Ba: 'The Superior treated me thickly; now I have betrayed him greatly.'
translations.idiomatic: He turned to Liu Ba and said: The throne has dealt generously with me, and I have repaid it with black ingratitude.'

sentences.zh: 我死，骸骨當暴。

translations.literal: 'When I die, bones and flesh ought to be exposed.'
translations.idiomatic: 'When I am gone, leave my bones for the sun and birds if you must.'

sentences.zh: 幸而得葬，薄之，無厚也。」

translations.literal: 'If fortunately I obtain burial, make it thin, not thick.'
translations.idiomatic: 'If they grant me a coffin after all, let the rites be spare—nothing rich or long.'

sentences.zh: 即以綬自絞死。

translations.literal: Immediately with the ribbon he hanged himself and died.
translations.idiomatic: Then he knotted his sash into a noose and strangled himself.

sentences.zh: 及八子郭昭君等二人皆自殺。

translations.literal: And eighth-rank Gu Zhaojun and two others all killed themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Two of his concubines of the eighth rank, including Gu Zhaojun, followed him into death.

sentences.zh: 天子加恩，赦王諸子皆為庶人，賜謚曰厲王。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven added grace, amnestied the King's sons all to commoners, granted posthumous title Prince Li.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor tempered justice with mercy: Xu's sons were spared but reduced to commoner rank, and the dead king was given the posthumous epithet Li, the Fierce King.'

sentences.zh: 立六十四年而誅，國除。

translations.literal: He was established sixty-four years and was executed; the state was removed.
